"Our union is wondrous and capable, and when our members are engaged, informed, and connected, there is no limit to what we can accomplish together."

My Experiences See My Platform How to Vote

What I Bring

Ralph is a Science teacher at Lowell High School who has spent nearly a decade in the classroom and in the community. He has taken lead roles in organizing, advocating, and showing up for the people around him. He knows what it takes to keep people informed and working toward the same goal — and he's ready to bring that energy to the Corresponding Secretary role.

Advocating on Capitol Hill
Education research conference panelist
UMass Lowell School of Education

I bring a data-driven background and the technology skills to build the communication infrastructure our union needs. As a Lowell educator, I have delivered multiple testimonies before the Massachusetts state legislature, served on several DESE committees. Beyond the district, I engage in education consulting and have worked as a Senior Policy Fellow and National Policy Board Member with Teach Plus. I currently serve as an adjunct professor of education at UMass Lowell, and have spent five years as an executive board member of CARE — a 501(c)(3) nonprofit focused on elevating youth civic engagement — where I have led the acquisition of over $200,000 to support youth programming and staffing.

Testimony at school committee
Supported students advocating to City Council about mold issues at Pawtucket Memorial Elementary School
Policy advocacy in Washington, D.C.

I have organized local and statewide community forums, organized the "No Kings" Lowell protests, facilitated professional development sessions, and worked directly with the union on subcommittee initiatives. I am capable and ready to do the work necessary to ensure the members of UTL 495 feel connected and supported.

Facilitating professional development
Ready to climb any mountain for our members

I give 110% to everything I commit to — ask anyone who has worked alongside me, or reach out to learn more!

Community Organizer — Lowell Education Justice Alliance & Indivisible Lowell 8th-Year Science Teacher Ph.D. Candidate, Educational Leadership — UMass Lowell Adjunct Professor of Education — UMass Lowell Teach Plus Senior Policy Fellow & National Policy Board Participates in Various DESE Committees Legislative Testimony — Local, State & Federal Data & Technology Skills Union Subcommittee Work
The Mission

Why I'm Running

I am running for Corresponding Secretary because I believe every member across every group deserves a union that communicates with them, fights for them, and ensures their voice is heard. I am proud to run on a shared vision of a stronger, more transparent, and more engaged UTL.

The role of Corresponding Secretary is fundamentally about communication, and communication must be a two-way street. When our members feel connected to their elected leaders, our union is even stronger.

We represent an increasingly diverse membership of teachers, paraprofessionals, custodial staff, cafeteria workers, IT, and security — and when every group knows that their voice shapes our direction, we build the kind of power that wins at the bargaining table.

I have the technological, organizational, and communication skills — and the passion — to build the systems that make that possible.

The Plan

What I Will Do

Ensure every member has a direct line and a support system — no one should wonder who to call, where to go, or whether anyone is listening. I will be accessible and responsive, and I will build the structures to make sure that is true across the entire union.

Strengthen the Building Rep Network

Communication should flow beyond email. Building reps should be the connective tissue between leadership and members — sharing survey results, delivering brief updates on negotiations, and holding short check-ins that keep every building informed and engaged.

Trimesterly Member Surveys

Launch a trimesterly member survey cycle to ensure every voice is captured — with results shared back to the membership within two weeks, every time, no exceptions. Survey data will also serve as a powerful lever in contract negotiations.

Member Handbook

Create a clear, accessible member handbook that outlines how to engage with the union, where to find information, who to contact, and your rights as a member.

Hybrid Member Meetings

Access to union spaces, including the negotiating room, should not depend on whether someone can physically be in a building at a specific time. Countless unions utilize online calls — so can we.

Champion Bread & Butter Issues

I will be a strong voice for protecting healthcare, securing pensions, and ensuring that retirees who gave their careers to this district are never forgotten in our advocacy. These are the foundations that allow our members to stay in the profession and retire with dignity.

The Challenge

The Biggest Hurdle in Our District

There is a disconnect between the decisions made at the central office level and what staff actually experience in their buildings. The working conditions, professional development, evaluations, and support systems are too often designed without meaningful input from the people most affected — which breeds distrust and turnover.

A strong union bridges that gap, and a strong Corresponding Secretary makes sure information flows in both directions so that no member is ever left in the dark.

A stronger community means a stronger collective bargaining unit.
Every initiative I am proposing serves that goal.

I am ready to do this work.
Take Action

How to Vote

The election takes place by US Mail. Here are the key dates to know:

Election Timeline

Ballots Mailed
By March 6, 2026
Ballots mailed to all members in good standing no later than the first Friday in March.
Deadline to Return
March 24, 2026
Ballots must be returned via US mail to the designated postal box by the fourth Tuesday in March.
Ballots Counted
March 24, 2026 — 5 PM
The Election Committee will open and count ballots at the UTL office. Members in good standing may observe.

Didn't receive a Notice of Election or a ballot?
Contact Nancy at the UTL: 978-937-9039
